storyInfo: As he tries to help an old friend with Oripathy, Eliseo realizes that he needs to work with others of the same mind if he wants to fundamentally improve conditions for the Infected.

attributes.content: You asked me why I chose your company. I would like to share an encounter with an old friend from when I was a social worker.
Next. Please put your health report here. What's your name?
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: Julio Blanco Antonio.
attributes.name: Anxious Patient

attributes.content: Julio?! It's you!
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: Glad to see you still remember me.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: It's not like I had many friends in middle school.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: I was worried when I lost touch with you after that thing. Where did you go? What have you been up to?
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: You mean that time when those kids cornered us in the bathroom and I broke one of their noses.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: The school wanted to expel me for it, but you insisted on appealing all the way up to the headmaster, and you actually got them to downgrade the expulsion to a suspension.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: But you chose to leave anyway...
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: I'm sorry, Eliseo. I was angry at how they treated descendants of refugees like us. I didn't want to stay and grovel to them.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: I was a fool. At least we could beat them in schoolwork while we were there.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: Julio... are you alright?
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: I... you know, Eliseo, that day in the bathroom. I looked down on you for trying to talk to them rather than fight them.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: But I began to admire you after learning what you did for me. Now, you're a valued member of the community, while I'm still a burden.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: I saw the health report in his hand. Saw it as soon as he walked through the door.
It's that bad? Your condition.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: The worst disease here is not Oripathy, but poverty.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: Stamp it for me, old friend. I hear the insurance companies need it before they pay. They accept your reports, don't they?
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: Is there something wrong?
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: I rarely see first-time visitors whose conditions are so serious, unless they refused treatment... or failed to receive a diagnosis until their condition had advanced significantly.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: When was your last health check? Where? Can I see the report?
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: I brought it... the last few checkups were at a community center near my home.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: This is... have you been taking your medicine on time?
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: I've taken all my meds on time! I swear! I never miss a single one!
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: But it works differently on different people, right? Oripathy wouldn't exist if there was a real cure!
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: Do you have to look so closely? It's just a regular old health report. Go ahead and stamp it if there's no problem, alright?
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: But your Cell-Originium Assimilation has increased by 1% this quarter alone. At this rate...
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: You need to consider additional treatment, if basic medication isn't working.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: Good advice. I'll keep it in mind.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: Julio... is this about money?
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: What else? Basic insurance is all we can afford.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: Nadie's pregnant. Yes, I got married. Before I got sick, I thought she was the only good thing to happen to me in this shithole country.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: Now, though, I'm a burden to my family.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: I'm sorry, Julio.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: I said I was sorry. At the time, I thought it couldn't be helped. Many patients see their conditions steadily deteriorate, until they meet the same end.
If you really want to help... do you have any meds here that you don't need? You know, leftovers, samples from insurance companies, expired ones, that sort of stuff?
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: If you could slip me a box or two...
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: I'm sorry, I can't. All medicine pickups must be registered.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: I've never begged you before, Eliseo, even when we were the best of friends. I hate begging, I-I never did that, even to those Columbian kids.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: ...I'm sorry, Julio.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: Hah, just kidding. I'll take my portion and leave.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: We collect donations from the community to subsidize treatment for patients with economic hardship. You can fill out this form...
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: Has anyone actually gotten that kind of help?
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: Yes... not many, though.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: Luck doesn't just decide to favor people like us.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: Fill it out anyway while I get your meds for this quarter. I'll be a minute.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: The door to the storage room was made of glass. As I unlocked the door with my pass, I saw him crane his neck looking at me in the reflection.
I felt guilty. Perhaps he still harbored hope that I would happen to find a spare box of medicine.
Here's your medicine, Julio... and I've stamped your health report. I'll do what I can to help you. I promise.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: Thanks, Eliseo... see you.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: At first, I thought little more of this reunion between old classmates, until an anxious colleague came to me the next morning.

Forty-five, forty-six... you're right, we're one box short. The number was correct when I took inventory yesterday before I left. You were the one who asked for another check, Mick. Did something happen?
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: Just one box? Well... I thought there might have been a break-in at the office. I lost my lanyard yesterday and found it in the corridor this morning.
attributes.name: Careless Social Worker

attributes.content: Don't look at me like this, I know you've reminded me many times...
attributes.name: Careless Social Worker

attributes.content: When you leave your lanyard on the table for everyone coming in and out of the office to see, we have no choice but to suspect the visitors when something goes missing. It's irresponsible to both sides.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: Yeah, yeah, I know. Did you notice any suspicious characters, then?
attributes.name: Careless Social Worker

attributes.content: Maybe someone stole the lanyard while they were in here, stole a box of meds, then fled the scene, leaving the work pass behind?
attributes.name: Careless Social Worker

attributes.content: That doesn't make sense. Why did the thief steal just one box? Could there be another reason?
attributes.name: Careless Social Worker

attributes.content: Let's not suspect anyone without evidence. Keep your pass safe, and I'll see what I can do about the lost meds.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: I visited Julio's house with a few daily necessities after work that day.
I know I shouldn't suspect him without evidence, but there was something about his sudden appearance and his condition that troubled me.
His expression when he saw me at the door was not one of welcome... it was hostility, mixed with a tingle of fear.
He opened the door just a crack, blocking it with his own body.
(Hushed) Eliseo? How did you...?
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: Your address was on the registration documents. I apologize for the surprise visit, but I was concerned. Are you and your wife doing alright?
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: (Hushed) Yes, yes... I'm fine, as you can see. Maybe I shouldn't have talked to you yesterday... anyway, there's no need to worry about us.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: (Hushed) It's a mess inside and we don't have anything for guests. Even the coffee beans are reused. I'm afraid we can't offer our hospitality...
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: At least let me say hello to your wife...
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: (Hushed) She's pregnant and the doctor says she needs rest. Go, Eliseo. Please.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: I heard a familiar name, honey. Is it that old classmate of yours?
attributes.name: Woman's Voice

attributes.content: You should be in bed! Eliseo is... he's leaving. I'm just going to see him off. No, there's no need to come...
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: Hello, Eliseo! I'm Nadie. Julio talks about you a lot. We really appreciate what you've done to help us. Please, come in and have some coffee.
attributes.name: Nadie

attributes.content: No...
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: That would be lovely, thank you.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: I'll get the pot, honey.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: You're the one who really needs rest. I'm not that weak.
attributes.name: Nadie

attributes.content: I can help myself.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: After a little bit of argument, Nadie brings out what little coffee they had left and poured me a cup. All they had in front of them was water. It was awkward, but the alternative was to let their kindness go to waste.
Julio speaks very highly of you. He thinks back to his middle school days when things aren't going well.
attributes.name: Nadie

attributes.content: He always put himself on the line for others. I owe him a lot myself.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: Yes, he does... speaking of which, I have to thank you for what you did to help us yesterday...
attributes.name: Nadie

attributes.content: Ahem! I told Eliseo that we were expecting. That's why he came today.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: Please don't interrupt me, dear. It's such an important thing, we ought express our gratitude properly.
attributes.name: Nadie

attributes.content: For what?
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: That box of medicine you gave us.
attributes.name: Nadie

attributes.content: I noticed the despair on Julio's face. He looked at me pleadingly.
Medicine...
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: It's nothing. Don't mention it.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: Oh, come now, I know how big a deal a whole box of medicine can be. But it's true that we really needed it.
attributes.name: Nadie

attributes.content: The baby isn't coming for another six months. We both want him or her to have a chance to meet Daddy.
attributes.name: Nadie

attributes.content: If Julio gets proper treatment...
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: ...It's getting late, Eliseo. I'll walk you to the station.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: What is this box of medicine about? What about the six months?
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: Please, Eliseo...
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: What's going on, Julio?
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: (Buries face in hands and weeps) Do you really have to do this?
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: Julio...
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: Nadie's infected as well... we worked on the same production line and got infected in the same accident...
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: With the amount of money an Infected can make, we hardly have anything left after insurance premiums.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: My situation is even worse. Besides, she's pregnant and needs to stay strong.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: We talked it over and came to the conclusion that this was the best choice. We only bought one insurance plan, and she takes the meds. That way, people won't know she's Infected, and she can find normal jobs that pay better.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: As for me...
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: Term life. One-time payout if the insured dies within one year...
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: The mother takes the medicine to ensure that the child is born safely, while you try to die within a year so they'll have the money to pay the bills and pay for treatment. That's your plan.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: At this rate, though, I may not last six months... the thought occurred to me when I heard that you were working in the community.
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: Other people might not be willing to stamp the papers, considering how quickly my condition is deteriorating. And I thought, maybe I could last a little longer if you managed to get me some meds...
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: I didn't want to cause you trouble, so I stole someone else's pass...
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: Now you know. I've told you everything. Please, for heaven's sake... don't report me... please...
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: I was at a loss for words. Father and Mother decided which one of them had to die to ensure that their child would live. Was this truly something that couldn't be helped?
It was for people like them that I chose this profession. But what could I do, at the end of the day, other than pay for that box of medicine out of my own pocket?
I made a decision after a few days of wrestling with frustration and helplessness. With Julio's permission, I began sending out petitions.
Letter to the Department of Business, Tower Mountains, unread.
Letter to Senator Scott, read, no response.
Letter to Mr. Roland, reporter, read. Expressed his sympathies in his response, but hinted that such cases were not newsworthy.
Letter to Trimounts Daily, read, declined.
I opened my mail every day, only to be greeted with disappointment. After dozens of fruitless efforts, I finally got a response from the Maylander Foundation.
Julio's story got a small column in a charity pamphlet, roughly the size of a train ticket. That was the turning point.
Am I speaking to Mr. Eliseo? I'm an aide to Mr. Scott, who read your article and is very sympathetic to your friend's plight. He would like to offer his assistance.
The senator...? I believe I wrote to him before.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: Ah... you must understand that Mr. Scott gets many requests from many sectors on a daily basis. I ask for your understanding if we were not timely in our response. But we're working toward the same goal now. All's well that ends well, right?
attributes.name: Senator's Aide

attributes.content: (Long exhale) Yes, indeed. What do I need to do?
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: Nothing except to provide us with your friend's contact information. We will reach out to him directly.
attributes.name: Senator's Aide

attributes.content: May I ask how Senator Scott intends to render assistance?
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: On television. Mr. Scott will be appearing on a show to talk about his charity projects.
attributes.name: Senator's Aide

attributes.content: This is Julio and Nadie's place.
attributes.name: Narrator

attributes.content: Like many second-generation Bolívarian immigrants, Julio and his wife live in an old, run-down building, getting by on backbreaking labor for paltry wages.
attributes.name: Narrator

attributes.content: But this night was different. The fate of this young couple was forever altered when an Originium boiler at their workplace exploded.
attributes.name: Narrator

attributes.content: (Exaggerated) Oh, honey... you're Infected too! But we'll make it, I swear. We've got each other, and that's a lot, isn't it?
attributes.name: Julio

attributes.content: *cough* There was actually some good news that I wanted to tell you... now, though, I don't know if it's good news or bad news.
attributes.name: Nadie

attributes.content: I'm pregnant.
attributes.name: Nadie

attributes.content: Julio seemed to have forgotten his lines, staring blankly at Nadie for a while, before he broke down in tears. Nadie tried to console him, but soon burst into tears as well.
It did not seem scripted. The cameraman quickly zoomed in onto their faces, and the footage was broadcast to the whole country, after being carefully and masterfully edited.
A TV outside Senator Scott's office played the program on loop, as patient after patient acted out their pain for the camera.
They had to showcase their despair and helplessness to the public, so that some benevolent benefactor might descend from above, and extend a helping hand...
It wasn't my place to criticize. They received help, help that I was unable to render. And so, I paid a visit to Senator Scott's office.
You said your name was Eli...seo?
attributes.name: Senator's Aide

attributes.content: You contacted me about the article seeking help for Julio and Nadie.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: Ah, you. What brings you in today, sir?
attributes.name: Senator's Aide

attributes.content: I watched the program that Julio was featured on. At the end, Mr. Scott promised to work with the community to reduce the burden on the Infected.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: I happened to be involved in this issue in the community, and I've come to discuss cooperation.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: Very good, very good... do you have an appointment?
attributes.name: Senator's Aide

attributes.content: No.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: Then I'm afraid you'll have to come again another day. Mr. Scott's schedule is booked solid.
attributes.name: Senator's Aide

attributes.content: Eliseo, welcome back.
attributes.name: Senator's Aide

attributes.content: I wrote to request an appointment, but I did not get an answer.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: As I've said before, Mr. Scott is a busy man, and he may not have time to read all the mail he gets.
attributes.name: Senator's Aide

attributes.content: That's why I've summarized my observations about the difficulties that the Infected face in this document. Can you pass it on to Mr. Scott?
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: Certainly.
attributes.name: Senator's Aide

attributes.content: Please tell him that I eagerly await his reply.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: I will, Eliseo.
attributes.name: Senator's Aide

attributes.content: If it isn't Eliseo again. You've been here once a week these past few months.
attributes.name: Senator's Aide

attributes.content: All I can do for them is persevere. But Mr. Scott can do much more.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: I agree. I will remind Mr. Scott to read the documents that you compiled. But he's out of office today, so I'm afraid you'll have to come again another time.
attributes.name: Senator's Aide

attributes.content: ...I will.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: You understand, don't you, Eliseo? A show can do a lot for Mr. Scott. But what does he have to gain from continuing to intervene? What can this community do for a senator?
attributes.name: Senator's Aide

attributes.content: Thanks for the explanation. I understand now... I won't bother you again.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: This is why I came to your company.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: Thank you for sharing this story, Eliseo. But you haven't answered why you came to Pure Heart Medical.
attributes.name: Pure Heart Medical HR Staff

attributes.content: From my point of view, your last statement indicates that you saw the politician for who he is, and stopped wasting time on him.
attributes.name: Pure Heart Medical HR Staff

attributes.content: Why turn to us, then? Is it because we were the only ones who answered you?
attributes.name: Pure Heart Medical HR Staff

attributes.content: My friend got the help he needed thanks to a show. At the end of the day, though, nothing has changed for the Infected.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: A whole family sharing a single person's prescription; patients who falsify blood tests to hide the progress of their condition; patients who choose to end their lives because of economic pressure and how other people look at them...
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: I can't prepare a show and find a benevolent sponsor for everyone...
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: I don't even know if my proposal works or not. If there's no one to open it, there's no one to criticize or approve it.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: I came to seek cooperation.
attributes.name: Eliseo

attributes.content: Would things be different if there was a whole group of people sharing the same goal, rather than just one or two futilely trying to change things on their own? I want to give it a try.
attributes.name: Eliseo
